com.mentor.chs.api

Interface IXWriteableObject

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXObject
    All Known Subinterfaces:

IXSpotTape, IXStructureNode, IXSubAssembly, IXSymbol, IXTerminal, IXText, IXTopologyDesign, IXVariantSignal, IXVariantWire, IXWire, IXWireEnd

    public interface IXWriteableObject
extends IXObject
    Interface used by those objects that can have their properties/attributes changed. Currently, you can only change properties/attributes from an IXAction.execute() method. For example: 
     boolean execute (IXApplicationContext context) {


    // Here we obtain the object that we wish to change the properties/attributes on.
    // This will usually be an object obtained from the current-design or the current
    // selection-set. The current-design or selection-set can be obtained from the
    // 'IXApplicationContext' passed in as a parameter to this method.
    IXObject xObject = …


    // Only some types of object are writeable.
    // Here we check to see if the type of object we have is writeable.
    if( xObject instance of IXWriteableObject ) {


       IXWriteableObject writeableObject = (IXWriteableObject) xObject;


       // We need to check that we are allowed to change this object.
       // Only objects in the current-design/selection-set will be changeable.
       IXAttributeSetter changeStore = writeableObject.getAttributeSetter();
       if(changeStore != null) {


          // This 'changeStore' object is used to store the changes you
          // want to make on the 'writeableObject' it was obtained from.
          // You will need to obtain a unique 'IXAttributeSetter' object
          // from all the objects you wish to change.


          // Here we are either adding a new property or changing the
          // value of an existing property called 'wibble'.
          changeStore.addProperty(“wibble”, “100”);


          // Changes are stored in the 'IXAttributeSetter' objects used and will
          // only be applied if this 'execute()' method returns 'true'.
          // If this method returns 'false', any changes stored in the
          // 'IXAttributeSetter' objects used will be discarded.


       }
    }


    // We need to return 'true' for the changes to be applied.
    return true;
 }
    See Also:
    IXApplicationContext, IXAction

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      IXAttributeSetter getAttributeSetter()
      Used if you need to change the value of a property or attribute of this object.

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.api.IXObject

        getAttribute, getAttributes, getBaseID, getID, getParentID, getProperties, getProperty, getSymbol, toHTML, toHTML

    • Method Detail

      • getAttributeSetter

        IXAttributeSetter getAttributeSetter()
        Used if you need to change the value of a property or attribute of this object. This method returns an 'IXAttributeSetter' that will store numerous changes to be applied. You should note that this method will return null if you are not allowed to change any values on this object in a particular situation.
        Returns:
        IXAttributeSetter that stored the changes to be applied when the IXAction.execute() method completes. It will return null if you are not allowed to change this object. You should check for a null return value.
